title: Lazarum johnsonianum
intro: Lazarum johnsonianum, also known as Johnson's root, is a plant species that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The health benefits of Lazarum johnsonianum include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain and swelling, and fighting infections. The bioactive constituents of Lazarum johnsonianum include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lazarum johnsonianum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical application.
